title: aethusa cynapium f. agrestis
intro: Aethusa cynapium f. agrestis, also known as wild hemlock or rough hemlock, is a plant species belonging to the carrot family and is a variety of the highly toxic Aethusa cynapium. It has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve rheumatism, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Aethusa cynapium f. agrestis include its potential to treat conditions such as urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and inflammation, although its toxicity and potential side effects must be carefully considered. Herbal preparations of Aethusa cynapium f. agrestis include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner due to the plant's potential toxicity.
